rationale: The cited paper is used as more than a background name-check: beyond the introductory literature list, it is directly referenced when the citing paper formulates the retailer's unconstrained category profit-maximization problem. That optimization formulation is central to the citing paper's modeling workflow and pricing recommendations. The citing study positions itself as extending grocery-based category pricing work into automotive aftermarket retailing, with Chintagunta (2002) serving as a key prior anchor in that line.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on Chintagunta (2002) as a methodological and conceptual precedent for retailer category pricing optimization, especially the profit-maximization setup. It then builds a store-level implementation in a new domain (automotive aftermarket) and validates it with field experimentation.
